Class Nonogramm

java.lang.Object
Nonogramm
public class Nonogramm extends Object
Represents a Nonogram (Picross) puzzle grid.

A Nonogram is a logic puzzle where rows and columns contain sequences describing groups of consecutive filled cells. The player fills the grid so that each row and column matches its sequence.

Cell encoding:

  • '?' — unknown / unassigned
  • '0' — empty (crossed)
  • '1' — filled (colored)

  • Constructor Details

    • Nonogramm

      public Nonogramm(ArrayList<ArrayList<Integer>> rowSequences, ArrayList<ArrayList<Integer>> colSequences)
      Constructs a new Nonogram puzzle.
      Parameters:
      rowSequences - sequences describing filled segments for each row
      colSequences - sequences describing filled segments for each column

  • Method Details

    • getSpecificRowSequence

      public ArrayList<Integer> getSpecificRowSequence(int row)
      Returns the sequence describing a specific row.
      Parameters:
      row - row index
      Returns:
      list of consecutive filled segment lengths
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if the row index is invalid

    • getSpecificColSequence

      public ArrayList<Integer> getSpecificColSequence(int col)
      Returns the sequence describing a specific column.
      Parameters:
      col - column index
      Returns:
      list of consecutive filled segment lengths
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if the column index is invalid

    • getRow

      public char[] getRow(int row)
      Returns the pixel values of a row.
      Parameters:
      row - row index
      Returns:
      character array representing the row
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if the row index is invalid

    • getCol

      public char[] getCol(int col)
      Returns the pixel values of a column.
      Parameters:
      col - column index
      Returns:
      character array representing the column
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if the column index is invalid

    • setPixel

      public void setPixel(int row, int col, char pixel)
      Sets a pixel value in the puzzle grid.
      Parameters:
      row - row index
      col - column index
      pixel - pixel value ('?', '0', or '1')
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pixel is not valid
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if row or column index is invalid

    • setPicture

      public void setPicture(char[][] picture)
      Replaces the entire picture grid.
      Parameters:
      picture - new grid state

    • printPicture

      public void printPicture()
      Prints the current puzzle grid to standard output.

    • pictureStripeAdheresSequence

      public static boolean pictureStripeAdheresSequence(char[] stripe, ArrayList<Integer> sequence)
      Checks whether a completed stripe (row or column) matches a given sequence.

      The stripe must not contain unknown cells ('?').

      Parameters:
      stripe - row or column to evaluate
      sequence - expected segment sequence
      Returns:
      true if stripe matches the sequence exactly

    • stripeSolvable

      public static char[] stripeSolvable(char[] stripe, ArrayList<Integer> sequence)
      Checks whether a stripe (row or column) could be completed to match a given sequence.

      The stripe can contain unknown cells ('?').

      Parameters:
      stripe - row or column to complete
      sequence - expected segment sequence
      Returns:
      null, if no solution exists, else a possible solution

    • isRowComplete

      public boolean isRowComplete(int row)
      Checks whether a row is completely and correctly filled.
      Parameters:
      row - row index
      Returns:
      true if the row matches its sequence

    • isColComplete

      public boolean isColComplete(int col)
      Checks whether a column is completely and correctly filled.
      Parameters:
      col - column index
      Returns:
      true if the column matches its sequence

    • isComplete

      public boolean isComplete()
      Determines whether the entire puzzle is solved correctly.
      Returns:
      true if all rows and columns satisfy their sequences
